exercises.title: Calling a Function
exercises.description: Functions package reusable code. Call the provided function multiple times with different arguments to see how it avoids repetition.
exercises.starterCode:
def print_separator(char, length):
    """Print a separator line made of the given character."""
    print(char * length)

# TODO: Call print_separator with '=' and 20
print_separator("=", 20)

# TODO: Call print_separator with '-' and 30
print_separator("-", 30)

# TODO: Call print_separator with '*' and 10
print_separator("*", 10)
hints: Call a function by writing its name followed by arguments in parentheses.
The function is already defined — you just need to call it.

exercises.title: DRY Principle in Action
exercises.description: This code repeats the same calculation three times. Define a function calculate_tip() and replace the repeated code with function calls.
exercises.starterCode:
def calculate_tip(bill, rate):
    """Return (tip_amount, total) for a given bill and tip rate."""
    tip = bill * rate
    total = bill + tip
    return tip, total

# TODO: Use calculate_tip() for each table instead of duplicating code
tip1, total1 = calculate_tip(45.50, 0.20)
tip2, total2 = calculate_tip(82.30, 0.18)
tip3, total3 = calculate_tip(120.00, 0.15)

print(f"Table 1: tip=${tip1:.2f}, total=${total1:.2f}")
print(f"Table 2: tip=${tip2:.2f}, total=${total2:.2f}")
print(f"Table 3: tip=${tip3:.2f}, total=${total3:.2f}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
Table 1: tip=$9.10, total=$54.60
Table 2: tip=$14.81, total=$97.11
Table 3: tip=$18.00, total=$138.00
hints: Call calculate_tip(bill, rate) and unpack the result: tip, total = calculate_tip(...).
The function is already defined — use it to avoid writing the same calculation three times.

exercises.title: Anatomy of a Function
exercises.description: Define a complete function with a docstring, parameters, body, and return value. The function should calculate the area of a rectangle.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Define a function rectangle_area(length, width)
# It should have a one-line docstring
# It should return length * width
def rectangle_area(length, width):
    """Calculate and return the area of a rectangle."""
    return length * width

# Test calls
print(rectangle_area(5, 3))
print(rectangle_area(10, 4))
print(rectangle_area(7, 7))
hints: def function_name(param1, param2): starts the definition.
Indent the body 4 spaces; use return to send back the result.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Why Functions?
warmup.description: Functions prevent code duplication (DRY principle). What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
def greet(name):
    print(f"Hello, {name}!")

greet("Alice")
greet("Bob")
greet("World")
warmup.expectedOutput:
Hello, Alice!
Hello, Bob!
Hello, World!
